title: NOC 21234 – Web Developers and Programmers | GTR Immigration
description: Web Developers and Programmers are in strong demand across Canada, particularly in digital hubs such as Toronto, Vancouver, and Montreal. Classified under NOC 21234 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for Canada’s skilled-worker immigration programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), employer-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)
classification.minor_group: 2123 – Computer, software, and web designers and developers

duties: Develop, write, modify, and test website-related code
Conduct quality, security, and UI testing
Collaborate on site architecture and design
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, web development, programming, or software engineering
Or a college diploma in a related IT field
job_outlook: This keeps Web Developers and Programmers immigration-viable, particularly for candidates with:
additional_information: Canada’s expanding IT sector, startup ecosystem, digital-marketing agencies, and e-commerce industry continue to fuel hiring for:
Full-stack developersFront-end and back-end specialistsWeb application engineersCMS developersDigital-platform managers
Typical annual salaries range from $34,700–$96,200 CAD, depending on experience, province, and technical specialization.
